cards_clickable.evidence: SignalCardFeatured and SignalCardStandard wrapped with <Link to=/intelligence/signals/:id> in IntelligenceFeedSection.tsx. Both variants have focus-visible ring for keyboard accessibility and aria-label on the Link.
files_changed: src/components/intelligence/IntelligenceFeedSection.tsx — Link import + wrap featured + wrap grid cards
src/App.tsx — added lazy import IntelligenceSignalDetail + Route /intelligence/signals/:id

detail_page.evidence: TanStack Query fetches market_signals WHERE id=:id. Loading=skeleton, error=retry+back link, 404=handled by error throw from supabase .single(). Renders: hero image, type label, direction glyph, title, description, confidence bar, impact score, related brands, topic, source + external link, AI provenance note.

cards_with_images.evidence: getSignalImage() applied to all SignalCardFeatured and SignalCardStandard renders. Priority: signal.image_url → signal_type map → vertical map → default SVG. All cards render with image — never blank. Image is lazy-loaded.

image_placement.featured: Full-width h-36 image strip above card content
image_placement.standard: h-24 image strip with accent bar overlay at bottom edge

cards_with_images.cards_with_images_sample: all (100% — every card gets an image, no card is left blank)

image_hook.note: Phase 1 implementation — static fallback. Phase 2 (IMAGE-INTEL-01): upgrade to query images table with signal.image_url FK when that WO runs.

layout_regression.evidence: No existing layout rules changed. Card className unchanged. Image containers use overflow-hidden to prevent layout bleed. If image fails to load, card still renders with background color fallback (bg-graphite/5).

notes: Chunk warning (vendor-docs >600KB) is pre-existing — mammoth + pdfjs-dist. Not introduced by this WO.
Images use existing static SVGs from /images/brand/photos/. No new assets required.
When IMAGE-INTEL-01 seeds an images table + image_url on market_signals, getSignalImage() will use signal.image_url first — zero code change needed at that point.
